title: NIS2 Business Continuity and Crisis Management Monitoring
exclusions: Does not certify legal conformity of the directive itself, file formal supervisory notifications, sign off board minutes, or replace qualified legal review of crisis communications.
evidence: Weekly saved search `UC-22.2.4`, Dashboard Studio panel export, RFC3161-qualified PDF or CSV bundle stored in the restricted evidence index with owner `Head of IT Operations`.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(c) requires business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management. The UC monitors backup job success rates and RPO/RTO trending in Splunk but cannot verify that restore tests produce usable data or that crisis-management plans were exercised, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Shows BCM plans, BIA reviews, and failover tests stay on schedule by matching ServiceNow GRC policies plus `category=bcp_review` tasks to `nis2_bcp_inventory`, and surfaces ITSI services that leave 'green' during `is_drill` exercises.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.2.4` on `index=itsm` (`sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy`, `sourcetype=snow:task`) and `index=itsi` `sourcetype=itsi:service_health`, enriched via `lookup nis2_bcp_inventory` (plan_id, owner, review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date); exports/PDF to vault with RFC3161. SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Insert a BCM policy row in ServiceNow whose `last_review_date` in `nis2_bcp_inventory` is 400 days old while `review_cycle_days` is 365, and confirm the alert fires naming `plan_key`, `plan_owner`, and the open `snow:task` number; during an ITSI drill set `is_drill=1` on a non-green service and verify the appended subsearch lists `service_name` with `unhealthy_drill_signals>0`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Update the inventory so `last_review_date` is within cycle, close related tasks to `Complete`, keep ITSI services green throughout the drill window, and confirm the scheduled search returns zero breach rows after the 7 a.m. Monday run while the audit dashboard still shows the last successful empty snapshot timestamp.

dataSources: `index=itsm` with Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) normalized pulls: `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` (plan identifiers, `sys_updated_on`, workflow state, policy classification) and `sourcetype=snow:task` for `category=bcp_review` work (task number, assignment group, closed_at, test_date). `index=itsi` with Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841): `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` (service_id, service_name, service_health_status, drill_window flags if tagged). CSV lookup `nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` joins plan_id to review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date, owner, and BIA refresh markers.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841); define `nis2_bcp_inventory` in `transforms.conf` on the Search Head
spl:
(index=itsm (sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy OR (sourcetype=snow:task category=bcp_review)) earliest=-180d@d) OR (index=itsi sourcetype=itsi:service_health is_drill=1 earliest=-24h)
| eval plan_key=if(index="itsm",coalesce(plan_id,u_bcp_plan_ref,cmdb_ci),service_name)
| lookup nis2_bcp_inventory plan_id AS plan_key OUTPUT plan_name, owner, review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date
| eval last_review_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_review_date),strptime(last_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval days_since_review=if(index="itsm" AND isnotnull(last_review_epoch),round((now()-last_review_epoch)/86400,1),null())
| eval review_breach=0
| eval review_breach=if(index="itsm" AND ((days_since_review>review_cycle_days) OR isnull(last_test_date) OR match(lower(state),"pending|waiting|work in progress")),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(index="itsi" AND NOT match(service_health_status,"(?i)normal|green"),1,review_breach)
| where review_breach=1
| stats latest(service_health_status) as svc_status latest(days_since_review) as days_since_review latest(owner) as plan_owner values(plan_name) as plans by plan_key index
| sort 0 - days_since_review
description: Correlates ServiceNow GRC and BCM task evidence with a BCP inventory lookup and ITSI service-health rows during declared drills, surfacing overdue plan reviews, missing failover test dates, stalled workflow states, and services that breach health expectations while `is_drill=1`.
value: Instead of chasing spreadsheets before an audit, executives get a dated list of which continuity artefacts are out of cadence and which practice runs actually stressed the production service tree — so gaps are fixed before regulators ask and crisis calls start with facts, not guesses.
implementation: (1) Install Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) and enable the GRC/policy and task pulls your process already uses; map `category=bcp_review` for BCM work. (2) Load `nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` via Lookup Editor or `lookups/` + `transforms.conf`. (3) Tag ITSI entities used in failover drills; ensure `itsi:service_health` includes `is_drill` during exercises. (4) Schedule the saved search weekly and push results to the compliance dashboard. (5) Archive outputs with RFC3161 timestamping per `evidenceSigning`.
visualization: Dashboard Studio row A: table of `plan_key`, `plan_owner`, `days_since_review`, BIA freshness flag from lookup. Row B: ITSI Service Analyzer deep link for drill services in breach. Row C: single value of open `snow:task` counts stuck >14 days.
knownFalsePositives:
**ServiceNow backlog grooming:** Distinguish long-lived `snow:task` items already waived by risk acceptance in the ticket. Suppress with `lookup nis2_bcp_exception_register exception_key AS number OUTPUT suppress_until | where now() > suppress_until OR isnull(suppress_until)`.

**Vendor field renames after upgrade:** Distinguish missing `plan_id` because the field moved to a new alias. Suppress nothing — fix `props.conf` FIELDALIAS in Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) until `plan_key` populates again.

**Planned maintenance on ITSI KPIs during drills:** Distinguish `service_health_status` red caused by known change windows from real failover failure. Suppress with `lookup maintenance_windows_drills service_name OUTPUT in_window | where in_window!=1` or a time-bound exception row.

**Historical imports inflating “days since review”:** Distinguish bulk-loaded policies with synthetic `last_review_date`. Suppress via filter `where source!="legacy_migrate"` after tagging those sources during onboarding.
equipmentModels: PagerDuty / Opsgenie integration (on-call and escalation data)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, major incident and crisis management data)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, notable events and incident tracking)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This UC implements NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) — business continuity, such as backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management. Unlike UC-22.2.24 (which tracks BCP/DR plan governance and exercise schedules), this UC monitors the real-time effectiveness of crisis management during actual incidents: response time, escalation adherence, communication completeness, and crisis resolution.
• **Crisis management vs incident response**: NIS2 distinguishes between security incidents (Art.23 — reporting) and business continuity events (Art.21(2)(c) — resilience). A ransomware attack is both: it's a security incident requiring Art.23 notification AND a business continuity event requiring crisis management. This UC focuses on the crisis management dimension: are the right people mobilised? Are communications sent? Is the crisis resolved within the defined timeframe?
• **Data sources**:
  - `index=notable` (Splunk ES) — notable events from correlation searches. Used to measure detection-to-response time (time from notable creation to first analyst assignment).
  - `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident` — ServiceNow major incidents (priority 1 and 2). Key fields: `number`, `priority`, `state`, `opened_at`, `resolved_at`, `assigned_to`, `assignment_group`, `business_impact`, `u_crisis_declared` (custom field — yes/no), `u_crisis_commander`.
  - `index=pagerduty sourcetype=pagerduty:incident` — PagerDuty incident events (creation, acknowledgement, resolution). Used to measure on-call response time.
  - `nis2_crisis_sla.csv` — crisis management SLAs. Columns: `priority` (P1, P2), `detection_to_response_min` (target minutes from detection to first responder acknowledgement — e.g., 15 for P1, 30 for P2), `response_to_command_min` (target minutes from response to crisis commander assignment — e.g., 30 for P1), `command_to_comm_min` (target minutes from crisis declaration to stakeholder communication — e.g., 60 for P1), `max_resolution_hours` (target hours for crisis resolution — e.g., 24 for P1).
  - `nis2_crisis_comm_register.csv` — stakeholder communication register. Columns: `stakeholder_role` (ceo, board_chair, legal, comms, nca — the national competent authority), `notification_method` (email, sms, phone), `sla_minutes` (time within which the stakeholder must be notified after crisis declaration).
• **NCA notification** (Art.23): if the incident is significant (Art.23(3) criteria), the entity must notify the CSIRT or competent authority within 24 hours (early warning), 72 hours (incident notification), and 1 month (final report). This UC tracks NCA notification timing as part of crisis communication.
• **RBAC**: role `nis2_crisis_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = notable, itsm, pagerduty, audit_evidence`. Crisis management data reveals the entity's response capability and weaknesses.
• **Index retention**: `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**ServiceNow major incidents:**
Configure the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow to pull P1/P2 incidents:
```ini
[snow_incident_p1p2]
table = incident
include_list = number,priority,state,opened_at,resolved_at,assigned_to,assignment_group,u_crisis_declared,u_crisis_commander,u_nca_notified_at
filter = priority<=2
timefield = sys_updated_on
id_field = number
interval = 300
index = itsm
```
The custom fields (`u_crisis_declared`, `u_crisis_commander`, `u_nca_notified_at`) must be added to the `incident` table in ServiceNow by the ITSM administrator.

Verify: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident priority<=2 earliest=-90d | stats count dc(number) as incidents values(state) as states`.

**PagerDuty integration:**
Install the PagerDuty App for Splunk or configure PagerDuty webhooks to send incident events to HEC. Key events: `incident.triggered`, `incident.acknowledged`, `incident.resolved`.

**Crisis SLA lookup:**
Place `nis2_crisis_sla.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-crisis/lookups/`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.4 NIS2 Crisis Management Effectiveness`.

Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

• **Three crisis management metrics**:
  - **Detection-to-response (D2R)**: time from the first notable event (detection) to the PagerDuty acknowledgement (response). Target: < 15 minutes for P1. Calculated as: `eval d2r_min=round((ack_epoch - notable_epoch) / 60, 1)`.
  - **Response-to-command (R2C)**: time from PagerDuty acknowledgement to crisis commander assignment in ServiceNow. Target: < 30 minutes for P1. Measured only for incidents where `u_crisis_declared=yes`.
  - **Crisis-to-communication (C2C)**: time from crisis declaration to stakeholder notification. For NCA notification per Art.23, the early warning must be within 24 hours.

• **SLA breach detection**: `lookup nis2_crisis_sla.csv priority OUTPUT detection_to_response_min | eval d2r_breach=if(d2r_min > detection_to_response_min, 1, 0)`. Each metric is compared against the SLA threshold.

• **Correlation between Splunk ES, PagerDuty, and ServiceNow**: the SPL joins across three data sources using time windows and incident identifiers. The ES notable event `rule_id` maps to the PagerDuty incident `dedup_key`, which maps to the ServiceNow `number` through a correlation lookup or naming convention.

Schedule: `0 * * * *` (hourly during active crises). For routine monitoring: `0 7 * * *` (daily). Trigger: `d2r_breach=1 OR r2c_breach=1 OR c2c_breach=1`.

Alert actions: (1) For D2R breach: email to the SOC manager — on-call response too slow. (2) For R2C breach: email to the crisis management team lead. (3) For C2C breach (especially NCA notification delay): email to the CISO and legal team — Art.23 compliance risk. (4) Write to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:crisis:evidence`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **D2R measurement**: create a test notable event in ES (via a test correlation search). Wait for PagerDuty to trigger and acknowledge. Run the UC. Verify `d2r_min` matches the actual delay.

(b) **Crisis declaration tracking**: create a test P1 incident in ServiceNow with `u_crisis_declared=yes` and `u_crisis_commander` assigned. Verify the UC detects the crisis and measures R2C.

(c) **SLA breach detection**: create a scenario where D2R exceeds the SLA (e.g., delay acknowledgement for 20 minutes with a 15-minute SLA). Verify `d2r_breach=1`.

(d) **NCA notification tracking**: set `u_nca_notified_at` in the test incident. Verify the UC calculates the time from `opened_at` to `u_nca_notified_at` and compares against the 24-hour early warning requirement.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Crisis Management`):
• **Row 1 — Crisis posture**: Single-value `Active P1/P2 incidents`. Single-value `D2R breaches (90d)` (red ≥ 1). Single-value `Median D2R (min)`. Single-value `NCA notifications pending` (red ≥ 1).
• **Row 2 — Incident timeline**: for active crises, a timeline showing: notable creation → PagerDuty trigger → acknowledgement → crisis declaration → commander assignment → stakeholder notification → resolution. Use Dashboard Studio's `splunk.timeline` or a custom visualisation.
• **Row 3 — SLA compliance table**: for the last 90 days, table of incidents with `number`, `priority`, `d2r_min`, `d2r_sla`, `d2r_breach`, `r2c_min`, `c2c_min`, `resolution_hours`.
• **Row 4 — D2R trend**: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:crisis:evidence earliest=-1y | timechart span=1mon median(d2r_min) as median_d2r`. Line chart showing response time trends.

Runbook (owner: Head of Crisis Management / CISO):
1. During active crises: monitor hourly. Ensure all stakeholders are notified within SLA. Track NCA notification timing carefully — Art.23 violations can result in administrative fines.
2. Post-crisis: conduct a lessons-learned review. Compare actual D2R/R2C/C2C against SLAs. Update the crisis management plan based on findings.
3. Monthly: review the SLA compliance table. Identify patterns in breaches (e.g., consistently slow D2R during night shifts).
4. Quarterly: present crisis management effectiveness metrics to the board.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **PagerDuty-Splunk time mismatch.** PagerDuty timestamps are UTC; Splunk ES notable events use the search head's timezone. Fix: normalise both to UTC epoch before calculating D2R: `eval notable_epoch=strptime(info_min_time, "%+")`.

• **ServiceNow custom fields not populated.** `u_crisis_declared` and `u_crisis_commander` are custom fields that may not be mandatory in the incident form. Operators skip them. Fix: make them mandatory for P1 incidents in ServiceNow's UI policy. Until then, treat null `u_crisis_declared` as "no" in the SPL.

• **PagerDuty dedup_key doesn't match ES rule_id.** The correlation between PagerDuty and ES depends on a shared identifier. Fix: configure the ES Adaptive Response action for PagerDuty to include the `rule_id` in the PagerDuty incident body or use a consistent `dedup_key` pattern.

• **No P1/P2 incidents in the measurement period.** If no crises occurred in 90 days, the UC produces zero results. Fix: this is a good outcome — no crises means no SLA breaches. Add a "no data" tile to the dashboard that says "No P1/P2 incidents in period — SLA compliance N/A." Separately, ensure crisis drills are conducted to validate the process (see UC-22.2.24).

• **NCA notification recorded in a different system.** Some entities track NCA notifications via email rather than ServiceNow. Fix: create a `nis2_nca_notifications.csv` lookup with `incident_number`, `notification_type` (early_warning, incident_notification, final_report), `sent_at`, `recipient_authority`. Merge with the ServiceNow data.
grandmaExplanation: We check that your emergency plans are reviewed on time, that practice runs are actually recorded, and that when you rehearse a failover the important services still look healthy. If something slips, you see it early instead of during an auditor visit.
